The three parameters of a PID controller — proportional gain (KP), integral gain (KI), and derivative gain (KD) — affect the closed-loop system differently. Proportional gain (KP) influences immediate response and steady-state error, while higher KP risks overshoot and instability. Integral gain (KI) addresses steady-state error but may introduce instability. Derivative gain (KD) reduces overshoot and oscillations, improving stability but slowing response. Selecting KP, KI, and KD requires balancing performance and stability.
